story.soThat: refresh token'ım invalidate olsun ve oturumum güvenli şekilde sonlansın
story.tasks: - Task 1: LogoutDto oluştur (AC: 2) - Subtask 1.1: src/modules/auth/dto/logout.dto.ts oluştur - Subtask 1.2: LogoutDto class-validator decorators ekle (@IsUUID, @IsNotEmpty) - Task 2: AuthService.logout() method implement et (AC: 3, 7, 8) - Subtask 2.1: src/modules/auth/auth.service.ts dosyasını düzenle - Subtask 2.2: logout(refreshToken: string) method oluştur - Subtask 2.3: Database'den refresh token lookup yap (prisma.refreshToken.findUnique) - Subtask 2.4: Token bulunamazsa → Idempotency için success dön (no error) - Subtask 2.5: Token bulunursa → prisma.refreshToken.delete() ile sil - Subtask 2.6: Return { success: true, message: "Logged out successfully" } - Task 3: AuthController.logout() endpoint oluştur (AC: 1, 4, 5, 6) - Subtask 3.1: src/modules/auth/auth.controller.ts dosyasını düzenle - Subtask 3.2: POST /auth/logout route tanımla - Subtask 3.3: @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard) decorator ekle (protected route) - Subtask 3.4: @Body() LogoutDto parametre ekle - Subtask 3.5: AuthService.logout() çağır - Subtask 3.6: Response dön: { success: true, message: "Logged out successfully" } - Subtask 3.7: Exception handling ekle - Task 4: Unit test yaz (AC: All) - Subtask 4.1: src/modules/auth/auth.service.spec.ts dosyasını genişlet - Subtask 4.2: Mock PrismaService - Subtask 4.3: Test: logout() with valid token deletes token from database - Subtask 4.4: Test: logout() with non-existent token returns success (idempotent) - Subtask 4.5: Test: logout() does not throw error for missing token - Task 5: Integration test yaz (AC: 1, 4, 5, 6, 7) - Subtask 5.1: test/auth-logout.e2e-spec.ts dosyası oluştur - Subtask 5.2: Test setup: Create test user, login to get tokens - Subtask 5.3: Test: POST /auth/logout with valid token → 200 OK, returns success message - Subtask 5.4: Test: Refresh token deleted from database after logout - Subtask 5.5: Test: Old refresh token cannot be used after logout (POST /auth/refresh → 401) - Subtask 5.6: Test: Access token still valid until expiry (protected route accessible) - Subtask 5.7: Test: POST /auth/logout with non-existent token → 200 OK (idempotent) - Subtask 5.8: Test: POST /auth/logout without JWT → 401 Unauthorized - Subtask 5.9: Test: Logout multiple times (idempotency test)

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. AC-2.5.1: POST /auth/logout endpoint oluşturulmuş ve çalışıyor (JWT guard ile korunmuş) 2. AC-2.5.2: Request body'de refreshToken (string) alınıyor ve validate ediliyor 3. AC-2.5.3: Refresh token database'den siliniyor (invalidate) 4. AC-2.5.4: Response döndürülüyor: { success: true, message: "Logged out successfully" } 5. AC-2.5.5: Logout sonrası eski refresh token kullanılamıyor (401 Unauthorized) 6. AC-2.5.6: Access token hala geçerli (expiry'e kadar, client-side discard expected) 7. AC-2.5.7: İdempotent operation (token bulunamazsa da 200 OK - no disclosure) 8. AC-2.5.8: Geçersiz refresh token → 404 Not Found (ama idempotency için 200 döndür)

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Authentication & Session Management
doc.snippet: POST /auth/logout endpoint (protected, JWT required). Refresh token deleted from database. Response: { success: true, message: "Logged out successfully" }. Idempotent operation (non-existent token returns success). MVP: Token blacklist yok, Phase 2'de Redis blacklist.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Authentication & Session Management
doc.snippet: Refresh Token Security: Database-backed (revokable), UUID format, rotation pattern, expiration checked. Idempotent Logout: No token existence disclosure, generic success message.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Authentication & Session Management
doc.snippet: Token Revocation Consistency: Logout idempotent, multiple requests safe. Concurrent logout handling via database unique constraints. No transaction needed for single delete operation.

doc.title: Boilerplate - Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: As a user, I want logout olabilmek, So that refresh token'ım invalidate olsun. JWT access token'ları stateless (blacklist MVP'de yok). Sadece refresh token invalidate. MVP: Token blacklist yok, Phase 2'de Redis blacklist.

doc.title: Story 2.4: Token Refresh
doc.snippet: RefreshToken Database Operations Ready: findUnique, delete patterns working. Idempotency pattern established. JwtAuthGuard available. Response format patterns established. Testing infrastructure ready.

artifact.reason: Controller where POST /auth/logout endpoint will be added. Shows @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard) pattern for protected routes and response format convention.
Service where logout() method will be implemented. Shows PrismaService injection pattern, error handling (UnauthorizedException), and idempotency patterns.
Similar DTO pattern for LogoutDto. Shows @IsUUID() and @IsNotEmpty() validators for refresh token string validation.
JWT Guard used to protect logout endpoint. Shows canActivate() method that validates JWT tokens and @Public() decorator bypass pattern.
Token service that handles refresh token operations. Reference for refresh token database patterns (findUnique, delete).
RefreshToken entity schema. Shows token field (unique), userID foreign key with Cascade delete, and indexed fields for performance.
E2E test reference showing test setup pattern: create user, login, test token operations, verify database state changes.

node.package: Core NestJS decorators (Controller, Post, Body, UseGuards, Injectable)

story-context.constraints: - MUST use JwtAuthGuard to protect POST /auth/logout endpoint (requires valid JWT) - MUST implement idempotent logout (non-existent token returns 200 OK, no error) - MUST use generic success message (no token existence disclosure for security) - MUST delete refresh token from database using prisma.refreshToken.delete() - MUST return response format: { success: true, message: "Logged out successfully" } - MUST follow RefreshTokenDto pattern for LogoutDto (class-validator decorators) - MUST NOT throw exception for non-existent token (idempotency requirement) - MUST NOT implement access token blacklist (MVP scope - stateless JWT pattern) - NO transaction needed (single delete operation is atomic) - Testing MUST cover idempotency (logout multiple times succeeds) - Testing MUST verify access token still valid after logout until expiry - Testing MUST verify old refresh token cannot be reused after logout

interface.name: POST /auth/logout
AuthService.logout
interface.signature: async logout(refreshToken: string): Promise<{ success: boolean; message: string }>

tests.standards: Testing infrastructure ready from previous stories. Unit tests use Jest with mock PrismaService. E2E tests use supertest with test database setup (jest-e2e-setup.ts). Follow existing auth test patterns: create user → login → test operation → verify database state. All tests must verify idempotency, error handling, and security requirements (generic error messages, no credential disclosure).
tests.locations: - Unit tests: src/modules/auth/auth.service.spec.ts - E2E tests: test/auth-logout.e2e-spec.ts (new file to create) - Test setup: test/jest-e2e-setup.ts - Test config: test/jest-e2e.json
tests.ideas: - Unit Test (AC-2.5.3, 2.5.7): logout() with valid token deletes token from database - Unit Test (AC-2.5.7): logout() with non-existent token returns success (idempotent) - Unit Test (AC-2.5.4): logout() returns correct response format - E2E Test (AC-2.5.1): POST /auth/logout requires JWT (401 without token) - E2E Test (AC-2.5.1, 2.5.2, 2.5.4): POST /auth/logout with valid token → 200 OK with success message - E2E Test (AC-2.5.3, 2.5.5): Refresh token deleted from database after logout, cannot be reused - E2E Test (AC-2.5.6): Access token still valid until expiry (protected route accessible) - E2E Test (AC-2.5.7): POST /auth/logout with non-existent token → 200 OK (idempotent) - E2E Test (AC-2.5.7): Logout multiple times (idempotency test) → all succeed - E2E Test (AC-2.5.2): Invalid refreshToken format → 400 Bad Request (validation error)
